description_long: This is a single-bladed axe featuring a broad, angular steel blade with a slightly curved cutting edge and a flat poll on the opposite side. The blade is securely mounted perpendicular to a straight, cylindrical wooden handle, which is dark brown in color and capped with black material at both ends. The handle appears smooth and polished, with no visible grip texture or ergonomic shaping, and is of medium length, suitable for one-handed or two-handed use. The blade is robust and utilitarian, lacking decorative elements, and is designed for chopping or splitting tasks. The mounting of the blade to the handle is traditional, with the blade fitted through the top of the handle and reinforced by the black end cap. No visible markings, engravings, or labels are present on either the blade or the handle.
description: A robust, single-bladed axe with a broad steel blade and a straight, polished wooden handle capped in black. The design is utilitarian and lacks decorative features.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the broad steel blade and the straight wooden handle with black end caps.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the cylindrical shape of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the blade and handle profile visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the handle's length and cylindrical form, with the blade edge barely visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical wooden handle with a smooth finish and black plastic or rubber end caps; no visible curvature or grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, single-edged steel blade with a slightly curved cutting edge and a flat poll; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted through the top of the handle and reinforced by an end cap.
